content.problemStatement: Johnny has invented a magic sequence. Each element of the sequence is defined by the same recursive definition - take some linear combination of previous elements (whose coefficients are fixed) and add to them the n-th powers of some integers. Formally: Xn = Xn-1*a1 + ... + Xn-i*ai + b1*d1n + ... + bj*djn, for some integer constants p,q,a1,...,ap,b1,..., bq,d1,..., dq. Of course, as the values can quickly grow, he computed them modulo a fixed value: 106. He wrote many consecutive values of the sequence, but then he lost most of his work. All he has now, is 10 consecutive values taken from somewhere in the sequence (he doesn't know at what n they begin), and the recursive rule. And he would like to recover the sequence, or at the very least, to be able to write the next 10 values taken from the sequence.
content.inputFormat: First line contains two integers p and q, followed by p coefficients a1 to ap, then q coefficients b1 to bq and their corresponding powers d1 to dq. Then, 10 consecutive values of the sequence Xn to Xn+9.
content.outputFormat: The output consists of the next 10 values in the sequence, Xn+10 to Xn+19.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The sequence is defined by the recursive formula Xn = Xn-1 + 1. The initial values are X0 = 11, X1 = 12, ..., X9 = 20. The next 10 values in the sequence are X10 = 21, X11 = 22, ..., X19 = 30.

The sequence is defined by the recursive formula Xn = Xn-1 + 2n. The initial values are X0 = 1, X1 = 3, ..., X9 = 1023. The next 10 values in the sequence are X10 = 2047, X11 = 4095, ..., X19 = 1048575.
The sequence is defined by the recursive formula Xn = Xn-1 + Xn-2. The initial values are X0 = 1, X1 = 1, ..., X9 = 55. The next 10 values in the sequence are X10 = 89, X11 = 144, ..., X19 = 6765.

solution.approach: The problem involves a recurrence relation with modular arithmetic. We can solve this by iterating through the recurrence relation and applying the modular arithmetic at each step.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 20) per test case, O(T * 20) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(20) — the dominant structure is the array to store the sequence values.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    p, q = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
    a = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    b = []
    d = []
    for _ in range(q):
        bi, di = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
        b.append(bi)
        d.append(di)
    x = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    for i in range(10, 20):
        sum = 0
        for j in range(p):
            sum = (sum + a[j] * x[i - j - 1]) % 1000000
        for j in range(q):
            sum = (sum + b[j] * (i + 1 - 10) * d[j]) % 1000000
        x.append(sum)
    print(' '.join(map(str, x[10:20])))
